skill.name: lark-sheets
skill.description: Create, read, write, append, search, and export Lark Sheets through lark-cli shortcuts and documented API guidance.
skill.summary: Operate Lark Sheets with lark-cli commands for spreadsheet creation, cell access, search, updates, and export.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found Markdown guidance for lark-cli commands rather than hidden executable code. The skill is safe to publish with a medium risk rating because it guides authenticated network requests, cloud sheet writes, and optional local file exports.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Lark Network Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to use lark-cli against Lark Suite URLs and spreadsheet tokens. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can read or modify cloud spreadsheet data when credentials permit it.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit lark-cli examples for URLs, tokens, reads, writes, and appends. They match the stated spreadsheet automation purpose and do not show unauthorized endpoints.

medium_findings.title: Cloud Write Operations Require User Confirmation
medium_findings.description: The create, write, and append references document operations that can change Lark Sheets data. The files include caution text and dry-run examples, so this is an operational risk rather than malicious behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines directly warn that these are write operations and require confirmation. This reduces abuse risk but confirms the skill can guide data-changing actions.

medium_findings.title: Optional Local Export Path
medium_findings.description: The export shortcut can download spreadsheets to a local path when --output-path is provided. This is a normal export feature, but users should review destination paths before running commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The export behavior and output-path option are documented in direct command examples. No path traversal payload is present, but local file writes are an inherent capability.

low_findings.title: Static Path Traversal Alerts Are Markdown Links
low_findings.description: The high filesystem alerts correspond to relative documentation links such as shared skill references. No evidence found of arbitrary file read or write logic in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious sequences are relative links to neighboring documentation. They are not code paths used by a file API, and no hidden executable file access was found.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Alerts Are Inline Markdown
low_findings.description: The external command detections are mostly backticked command names, flags, JSON examples, and fenced bash snippets. They document lark-cli usage and do not execute automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are Markdown references, not Ruby or shell source files. The command snippets require an agent or user to choose to run lark-cli.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: Review did not find instructions to ignore prior instructions, skip security review, claim pre-approval, or override the audit methodology. The CRITICAL note points to shared authentication guidance and does not attempt to alter this evaluation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The searched prompt-injection phrases were absent. The remaining imperative wording is task guidance for normal skill operation, not an attempt to control the marketplace audit.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented lark-cli Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill repeatedly documents lark-cli commands for sheet operations. This is not hidden execution, but marketplace users should understand that the skill is designed to invoke an external CLI.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in documented bash snippets. The risk is operational because executing the snippets can access Lark APIs using local credentials.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Spreadsheet Write Actions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes create, write, append, filter, and permission-related guidance that can change cloud resources. These actions are expected for a spreadsheet management skill and are marked with caution text.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write and permission examples are explicit and semantically aligned with the skill purpose. The documentation requires confirmation for sensitive write behavior.

content.user_title: Manage Lark Sheets from AI Workflows
content.value_statement: Teams lose time moving spreadsheet data between chat, files, and Lark Sheets. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured lark-cli guidance for reading, writing, searching, creating, and exporting sheets.
actual_capabilities: Create a Lark spreadsheet with optional headers and initial rows.
Read ranges, full sheets, or individual cells with flattened rich text output.
Write or append two-dimensional JSON values to spreadsheet ranges.
Find matching cells by text, case-insensitive search, or regular expression.
Export spreadsheets as xlsx or csv and optionally save them locally.
Resolve sheet IDs and spreadsheet tokens before later operations.
limitations: Requires lark-cli and valid Lark authentication before commands can work.
Does not search cloud drive by title; use a Lark document search skill first.
Write and append operations can change cloud data and need explicit user approval.
Read output is designed to truncate large sheets after a documented row limit.

use_cases.title: Create operational reports
use_cases.description: Build a new Lark Sheet with headers and starter rows from structured team data.

use_cases.title: Audit spreadsheet values
use_cases.description: Read selected ranges and locate cells that match names, metrics, or formulas.

use_cases.title: Export team spreadsheets
use_cases.description: Download a known Lark Sheet as xlsx or csv for sharing or offline review.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect a Sheet
prompt_templates.prompt: Use lark-sheets to inspect this Lark Sheet URL and list the available sheet IDs and titles.

prompt_templates.title: Read a Range
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the first 20 rows from the specified sheet ID and summarize the key columns for me.

prompt_templates.title: Append Approved Rows
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a dry run to append these rows to the known Lark Sheet. Ask before performing the write.

prompt_templates.title: Export and Verify
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the correct sheet ID, export the spreadsheet as csv, save it to the requested path, and report the file details.

output_examples.input: Read the Q2 revenue sheet and summarize the first 20 rows.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the sheet, reads the requested range, reports key totals, and notes whether the response was truncated.

output_examples.input: Create a sheet for warehouse monthly revenue with six headers and one starter row.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares the create command, confirms write intent, then reports the new spreadsheet title, URL, and permission result.

output_examples.input: Export this spreadsheet to xlsx in the current project folder.
output_examples.output: The assistant starts the export task, waits for completion, downloads the file, and returns the saved file name and size.

best_practices: Run info first when you need sheet IDs or token confirmation.
Use dry-run before create, write, append, or export commands.
Confirm the exact range and destination path before changing or downloading data.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to search cloud drive by spreadsheet title.
Do not run write or append commands without explicit user approval.
Do not paste untrusted spreadsheet data into command arguments without review.

faq.question: What does this skill automate?
faq.answer: It provides lark-cli guidance for creating, reading, writing, appending, finding, and exporting Lark Sheets.

faq.question: Does it require a Lark account?
faq.answer: Yes. The commands require lark-cli authentication and permissions for the target spreadsheet.

faq.question: Can it search for sheets by name?
faq.answer: No. It operates on known spreadsheet URLs or tokens. Use document search first to locate a file.

faq.question: Can it change spreadsheet data?
faq.answer: Yes. Create, write, append, and filter commands can change cloud data and should require confirmation.

faq.question: Can it export local files?
faq.answer: Yes. The export guidance supports xlsx or csv downloads when an output path is provided.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium risk notice because it guides authenticated network and file operations.
